title: Upstream validation: ingested values match the source tool within tolerance + provenance-coverage test
context: The ingestion task (incidence-explorers-ingest-007) produces a provenanced dataset for the pilot site. Before any dashboard or explainer can use those numbers, they must be validated against the upstream published tools (SEER*Explorer, GLOBOCAN/GCO) to confirm accuracy. Discrepancies must be investigated, not silently accepted. A provenance-coverage CI gate must also be wired in: every DataPoint must resolve to a Source or the build fails.
objective: Implement an upstream validation suite that (1) compares ingested DataPoint values against the upstream source tool (SEER*Explorer / GCO) within a documented numerical tolerance, flags and investigates any discrepancy rather than accepting it silently, and (2) adds a provenance-coverage CI gate: 100% of DataPoints must resolve to a Source — a DataPoint with no resolvable Source fails CI ('no source, no render'). Both checks must be wired into CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Ingested values are compared against the upstream tool (SEER*Explorer / GCO) within a documented tolerance; each comparison is recorded with source, value, upstream value, and delta
Any discrepancy outside the documented tolerance is flagged as a test failure and investigated before being accepted; discrepancies are not silently ignored
Provenance-coverage CI gate: 100% of DataPoints in the ingested dataset resolve to a Source record; a DataPoint without a resolvable Source fails CI
Both the upstream-validation suite and the provenance-coverage gate run in CI and fail the build on failure
Reviewed and signed off by the Statistics reviewer and Data-governance reviewer (recorded)
output: A merged PR implementing the upstream validation suite and the provenance-coverage CI gate, with green CI — ensuring ingested values are accurate against the upstream tool and every statistic has a resolvable source before it can render.
